edge: Quantization of large language models (LLMs) is the main focus of the context, addressing challenges due to outlier activations.
Outlier activations are a major challenge in achieving efficient low-bit representation in language models.
There are different types of outliers in the context, including Normal Outliers and Massive Outliers, affecting the model's performance.
DuQuant is introduced as a novel approach that utilizes rotation and permutation transformations to mitigate outliers effectively.
A rotation matrix is constructed using outlier dimensions as prior knowledge to redistribute outliers and enhance model performance.
Zigzag permutation is employed to balance the distribution of outliers across blocks, reducing block-wise variance.
DuQuant simplifies the quantization process and excels in managing outliers, improving model performance.
DuQuant outperforms the state-of-the-art baselines across various sizes and types of LLMs on multiple tasks, including 4-bit weight-activation quantization.
Natural language processing tasks can present deployment challenges on edge devices due to their large number of parameters.
Memory usage is a deployment challenge for natural language processing tasks due to the billions of parameters they have.
Inference speed is another deployment challenge for natural language processing tasks due to their numerous parameters.
Network quantization methods are explored to minimize memory usage by converting floating-point parameters into low-bit formats.
Network quantization methods aim to expedite inference by quantizing both activations and weights for accelerating the matrix multiplication process.
Presence of activation outliers can enlarge the quantization step sizes and cause significant accuracy loss.
Current research has developed methods to address Normal Outliers in activations to mitigate accuracy loss.
Besides Normal Outliers, there exists another type of activation outlier known as Massive Outliers.

Theoretical analysis confirms that these transformations greatly mitigate quantization challenges induced by outliers.
Permutation transformations greatly mitigate quantization challenges induced by outliers.
DuQuant offers several clear advantages over QuaRot, such as optimal rotation matrices and unique zigzag permutations.
DuQuant's optimal rotation matrix surpasses QuaRot's Hadamard rotation in managing outliers.
DuQuant's zigzag permutation significantly reduces activation variance across blocks, providing a distinct advantage for handling massive outliers.
DuQuant avoids time-consuming GPTQ algorithm in QuaRot by jointly smoothing weights and activations.
DuQuant significantly outperforms existing 4-bit weight-activation quantization baselines across various benchmarks, enhancing the efficiency and capacity of quantized LLMs.
DuQuant achieves a 5% improvement in Commonsense QA tasks and a 10% increase in zero-shot MMLU benchmarks for the Vicuna-v1.5-13B LLaMA model sizes.
In practical applications with the LLaMA2-7B model, DuQuant accelerates pre-willing phase and reduces memory usage during decoding phase, with minimal impact on performance.
DuQuant's effectiveness in enhancing the efficiency and capacity of quantized LLMs is highlighted by extensive evaluations.
Previous works have highlighted the challenge of normal outliers and massive outliers.
Activation outliers in LLMs pose a challenge for model compression, as highlighted in previous works.
Normal Outliers are outlier features in LLMs with large values across specific feature dimensions and present in all token sequences.
The primary distinctions between Normal Outliers and Massive Outliers are that Normal outliers persist across all token sequences, while Massive outliers are confined to a limited number of tokens.
Massive Outliers are a distinct type of outlier observed in LLMs, which exhibit significantly larger magnitudes than Normal Outliers.
Massive Outliers exist at the input of the down-projection layer within the FFN module, as discovered in contrast to previous studies observing massive outliers at the output of Transformer blocks.
The down-projection layer is within the FFN module, where massive outliers exist at the input, as depicted in Figure 1.

The zigzag permutation is introduced to minimize variance and further reduce the overall outliers effectively.
A zigzag sequence assigns channels with the highest activations to the first block and continues in descending order to subsequent blocks until the end of block K.
Upon reaching the final block, the order reverses, starting from the channel with the next highest activation and proceeding in ascending order.

The DuQuant method can simultaneously smooth the weight matrix. While the commonly adopted smooth technique is effective, it can cause the weight matrix of the down-projection layer to exhibit pronounced outliers, leading to performance degradation

In the proposed DuQuant method, the rotation transformation is applied to not only the activation input but also the weight matrix. The outliers induced by the smooth technique can be mitigated through our approximated rotation matrix, yielding a smoother, more quantization-friendly weight matrix
Moreover, this approach eliminates the reliance on complex weight quantization techniques, such as GPTQ used in Atom and QuaRot

DuQuant is an innovative quantization strategy for large language models (LLMs) that addresses the challenge of outlier activations by integrating rotation and permutation transformations.
DuQuant strategically redistributes outliers, effectively mitigating the impacts of both massive and normal outliers.
DuQuant simplifies the quantization process by strategically redistributing outliers, leading to substantial improvements in model performance.
DuQuant establishes new state-of-the-art results in 4-bit weight-activation quantization scenarios.
DuQuant enhances the deployment of efficient LLMs in resource-constrained environments.
